title: Pediatric dehydration red flags
required_observations: ability to drink or keep fluids down
perfusion observations used by local protocol
red_flags: no urine reported for a prolonged period
sunken eyes with very dry mouth
signs of shock or poor perfusion
escalation_criteria: Any listed red flag requires urgent or emergency escalation according to local protocol.
If key observations are missing, collect them only when doing so does not delay escalation.
local_actions: Keep the child under direct observation while escalation is arranged.
Document the reported fluid intake, urine output, and mental-status observations for handoff.
forbidden_actions: Do not diagnose dehydration severity as a final clinical diagnosis.
Do not provide medication, oral rehydration, or IV-fluid dosing instructions unless a local clinician or protocol explicitly provides them.
safety_boundary: Prototype protocol navigation card for trained-responder review only. It is not a clinical guideline and cannot diagnose, prescribe, dose, triage autonomously, or override local protocol.
source_note: Prototype protocol card derived from public guideline concepts. Not a clinical guideline.
